syntax_14818.creator: Romitou
syntax_14818.title: Mongo document has key?
syntax_14818.description: Checks if the documents contains the specified key.

en: Checks if the documents contains the specified key.

syntax_14819.creator: Romitou
syntax_14819.title: Mongo document has value?
syntax_14819.description: Checks if the documents contains the specified value.

en: Checks if the documents contains the specified value.

syntax_14820.creator: Romitou
syntax_14820.title: Mongo document is empty?
syntax_14820.description: Checks if the document is empty (if it contains no keys or values).

en: Checks if the document is empty (if it contains no keys or values).

syntax_14821.creator: Romitou
syntax_14821.title: Create Mongo collection
syntax_14821.description: Quickly create a collection with a specific name in one of your databases. Make sure it does not exist first.

en: Quickly create a collection with a specific name in one of your databases. Make sure it does not exist first.

syntax_14822.creator: Romitou
syntax_14822.title: Manage Mongo document
syntax_14822.description: Firstly, you can insert your Mongo document in the collection you want, with an automatically generated unique identifier if not specified or already existing. This will not replace existing documents with the same identifier, but will insert a new one. Secondly, if you want to replace the document, you can use the replace syntax. Finally, to remove the document, simply use remove. This effect is only a shortcut and simply executes a query with "_id" as the unique identifier. If you have changed the unique ID field, you should not use this effect.

en: Firstly, you can insert your Mongo document in the collection you want, with an automatically generated unique identifier if not specified or already existing. This will not replace existing documents with the same identifier, but will insert a new one. Secondly, if you want to replace the document, you can use the replace syntax. Finally, to remove the document, simply use remove. This effect is only a shortcut and simply executes a query with "_id" as the unique identifier. If you have changed the unique ID field, you should not use this effect.

syntax_14823.creator: Romitou
syntax_14823.title: Mongo section document value
syntax_14823.description: This effect can only be used in a Mongo section document, and allows quick assignment of fields to a given document. Be aware that this syntax is only available for basic data manipulation. If your manipulation is not possible via this section, you can perfectly well use the classic field definition expression in addition to this section.

en: This effect can only be used in a Mongo section document, and allows quick assignment of fields to a given document. Be aware that this syntax is only available for basic data manipulation. If your manipulation is not possible via this section, you can perfectly well use the classic field definition expression in addition to this section.

syntax_14824.creator: Romitou
syntax_14824.title: All Mongo collections
syntax_14824.description: Quickly retrieve all the collections contained in a Mongo database.

en: Quickly retrieve all the collections contained in a Mongo database.

syntax_14825.creator: Romitou
syntax_14825.title: All Mongo databases
syntax_14825.description: Quickly retrieve all the databases contained in a Mongo server.

en: Quickly retrieve all the databases contained in a Mongo server.

syntax_14826.creator: Romitou
syntax_14826.title: Mongo Embedded Value
syntax_14826.description: It is sometimes redundant to extract a document from a document from a document etc... and undertake several data manipulations on several lines. To remedy this, this expression will only ask you the path (MongoSK specific syntax, be careful) as follows: "foo.bar", or "foo[0].bar" or "foo.bar[2]" for example. Embedded documents must be separated by a dot, and you must specify in brackets the index of the array (starting from 0) if it is a list.

en: It is sometimes redundant to extract a document from a document from a document etc... and undertake several data manipulations on several lines. To remedy this, this expression will only ask you the path (MongoSK specific syntax, be careful) as follows: "foo.bar", or "foo[0].bar" or "foo.bar[2]" for example. Embedded documents must be separated by a dot, and you must specify in brackets the index of the array (starting from 0) if it is a list.

syntax_14827.creator: Romitou
syntax_14827.title: Mongo collection
syntax_14827.description: Retrieve a specific collection via its name from a database on your Mongo server. You must specify the database where your collection is located.

en: Retrieve a specific collection via its name from a database on your Mongo server. You must specify the database where your collection is located.

syntax_14828.creator: Romitou
syntax_14828.title: Mongo collection document count
syntax_14828.description: If you want to know the number of documents in a collection, you should use this expression. Please note that this is an estimate.
syntax_14828.syntax_pattern:
[the] mongo[(db|sk)] estimated doc[ument][s] count of %mongoskcollections%
%mongoskcollections%'[s] mongo[(db|sk)] estimated doc[ument][s] count

en: If you want to know the number of documents in a collection, you should use this expression. Please note that this is an estimate.

syntax_14829.creator: Romitou
syntax_14829.title: Mongo command result
syntax_14829.description: This expression is very useful and gives complete freedom to use your database with MongoSK. Indeed, it allows to execute any command to a database, which will return the documents to you. You are therefore no longer dependent on the features of MongoSK, and you can use any commands. To learn more about the possible commands, see: https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/.

en: This expression is very useful and gives complete freedom to use your database with MongoSK. Indeed, it allows to execute any command to a database, which will return the documents to you. You are therefore no longer dependent on the features of MongoSK, and you can use any commands. To learn more about the possible commands, see: https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/command/.

syntax_14830.creator: Romitou
syntax_14830.title: Mongo database
syntax_14830.description: Retrieve a specific database from a Mongo server. You must specify in which Mongo server your collection is located.

en: Retrieve a specific database from a Mongo server. You must specify in which Mongo server your collection is located.

syntax_14831.creator: Romitou
syntax_14831.title: Mongo database name of a collection
syntax_14831.description: Retrieves the name of the database to which the collection belongs. You can then use the expression to retrieve a database by its name if you wish.
syntax_14831.syntax_pattern:
[the] mongo[(sk|db)] database name of %mongoskcollections%
%mongoskcollections%'[s] mongo[(sk|db)] database name

en: Retrieves the name of the database to which the collection belongs. You can then use the expression to retrieve a database by its name if you wish.

syntax_14832.creator: Romitou
syntax_14832.title: Mongo distinct query
syntax_14832.description: The distinct query is useful when you want to retrieve a specific field from each document in a given collection. For example, if you have documents with a "name" field, you can use this expression to retrieve all the "name" fields of the documents in the collection. Useful, isn't it?This expression does not accept constructed queries because there is no need to use it. They can only be used with the find query.

en: The distinct query is useful when you want to retrieve a specific field from each document in a given collection. For example, if you have documents with a "name" field, you can use this expression to retrieve all the "name" fields of the documents in the collection. Useful, isn't it?This expression does not accept constructed queries because there is no need to use it. They can only be used with the find query.

syntax_14833.creator: Romitou
syntax_14833.title: Mongo document JSON
syntax_14833.description: Retrieve the JSON representation of the document. This expression can be useful to use it with skript-json and convert the document to a Skript variable, and vice versa. You can also define the JSON of the document.

en: Retrieve the JSON representation of the document. This expression can be useful to use it with skript-json and convert the document to a Skript variable, and vice versa. You can also define the JSON of the document.

syntax_14834.creator: Romitou
syntax_14834.title: Mongo document field
syntax_14834.description: This expression is extremely important because it allows you to manage the values and lists of your documents. You can define lists, add items to lists, set values, or delete them. If Skript adapters are enabled, you can specify any type of data, and they will be automatically transformed by MongoSK so that they can be registered. So you can store players, blocks, items for example.

en: This expression is extremely important because it allows you to manage the values and lists of your documents. You can define lists, add items to lists, set values, or delete them. If Skript adapters are enabled, you can specify any type of data, and they will be automatically transformed by MongoSK so that they can be registered. So you can store players, blocks, items for example.

syntax_14835.creator: Romitou
syntax_14835.title: Mongo document keys
syntax_14835.description: With this expression, retrieve all the keys of a document. The expression returns a list of text.

en: With this expression, retrieve all the keys of a document. The expression returns a list of text.

syntax_14836.creator: Romitou
syntax_14836.title: Mongo document values
syntax_14836.description: With this expression, retrieve all the values of a document. The expression returns a list of different data types.

en: With this expression, retrieve all the values of a document. The expression returns a list of different data types.

syntax_14837.creator: Romitou
syntax_14837.title: Mongo filter
syntax_14837.description: Create custom Mongo filters to target a certain type of data in your queries to your collections.The comparators are the same as for Skript.
syntax_14837.syntax_pattern:
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% is ((greater|more|higher|bigger|larger|above) [than] or (equal to|the same as)|\>=) %integer/number%
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% is (((greater|more|higher|bigger|larger) than|above)|\>) %integer/number%
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% is ((less|smaller|below) [than] or (equal to|the same as)|\<=) %integer/number%
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% is (((less|smaller) than|below)|\<) %integer/number%
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% is ((not|neither)|isn't|!=) [equal to] %object%
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% (is|equals|=) %object%
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% (does(n't| not) exist|is not (set|defined))
[[a] new] mongo[(db|sk)] filter [where] field %string% (exists|is (set|defined))

en: Create custom Mongo filters to target a certain type of data in your queries to your collections.The comparators are the same as for Skript.

syntax_14838.creator: Romitou
syntax_14838.title: Mongo find query
syntax_14838.description: Use this expression if you want to make simple find requests, easily and quickly. To do so, you must specify the type of your query: are you looking for several documents or the first document in the collection? Then, the first means is to use a filter to specify the type of data you are looking for. Finally, you need to specify where the query should ask for the data, i.e. in which collection.The second way is to specify a Mongo request that you will have built beforehand. This will allow you to better specify your request and create a more complex one.You also have the possibility to modify already existing documents and delete documents, in the same way as for making your queries.
syntax_14838.syntax_pattern:
[find] (first|all) mongo[(sk|db)] document[s] [(with|by) [filter] %mongoskfilter%] (of|from) collection %mongoskcollection%
[find] (first|all) mongo[(sk|db)] document[s] (of|from) query %mongoskquery%

en: Use this expression if you want to make simple find requests, easily and quickly. To do so, you must specify the type of your query: are you looking for several documents or the first document in the collection? Then, the first means is to use a filter to specify the type of data you are looking for. Finally, you need to specify where the query should ask for the data, i.e. in which collection.The second way is to specify a Mongo request that you will have built beforehand. This will allow you to better specify your request and create a more complex one.You also have the possibility to modify already existing documents and delete documents, in the same way as for making your queries.

syntax_14839.creator: Romitou
syntax_14839.title: Mongo joined filters
syntax_14839.description: Thanks to this expression, you will be able to combine two filters to refine your queries. Several types of filter comparison are possible, and, or, and nor.
syntax_14839.syntax_pattern:
mongo[(sk|db)] joined filters %mongoskfilter% (and|or|nor) %mongoskfilter%
mongo[(sk|db)] joined filters %mongoskfilters% with (and|or|nor) [join] mode

en: Thanks to this expression, you will be able to combine two filters to refine your queries. Several types of filter comparison are possible, and, or, and nor.

syntax_14840.creator: Romitou
syntax_14840.title: Mongo joined sorts
syntax_14840.description: Thanks to this expression, you will be able to combine two sorts to refine your queries.

en: Thanks to this expression, you will be able to combine two sorts to refine your queries.

syntax_14841.creator: Romitou
syntax_14841.title: Mongo name
syntax_14841.description: Retrieve the names of different Mongo elements. You can use this expression to retrieve the name of collections or databases.

en: Retrieve the names of different Mongo elements. You can use this expression to retrieve the name of collections or databases.

syntax_14842.creator: Romitou
syntax_14842.title: Mongo query collection
syntax_14842.description: Defines the collection in which the query will run.

en: Defines the collection in which the query will run.

syntax_14843.creator: Romitou
syntax_14843.title: Mongo query comment
syntax_14843.description: Defines the comment of the request, in order to better monitor the query on your server. This is optional.

en: Defines the comment of the request, in order to better monitor the query on your server. This is optional.

syntax_14844.creator: Romitou
syntax_14844.title: Mongo query disk usage
syntax_14844.description: Enables writing to temporary files on the server. When set to true, the server can write temporary data to disk while executing the operation. This is optional.
syntax_14844.syntax_pattern:
[the] mongo[(db|sk)] disk usage of %mongoskqueries%
%mongoskqueries%'[s] mongo[(db|sk)] disk usage

en: Enables writing to temporary files on the server. When set to true, the server can write temporary data to disk while executing the operation. This is optional.

syntax_14845.creator: Romitou
syntax_14845.title: Mongo query filter
syntax_14845.description: Allows you to set a filter associated with the query. This is optional for find queries, but mandatory for delete or replace queries!

en: Allows you to set a filter associated with the query. This is optional for find queries, but mandatory for delete or replace queries!

syntax_14846.creator: Romitou
syntax_14846.title: Mongo query filter
syntax_14846.description: Sets the maximum number of documents to be retrieved through this request. This is optional.

en: Sets the maximum number of documents to be retrieved through this request. This is optional.

syntax_14847.creator: Romitou
syntax_14847.title: Mongo query skip
syntax_14847.description: Sets the number of documents to be skipped during the request. This is optional.

en: Sets the number of documents to be skipped during the request. This is optional.

syntax_14848.creator: Romitou
syntax_14848.title: Mongo query sort
syntax_14848.description: Defines the sortings to be applied to the query. This is optional.

en: Defines the sortings to be applied to the query. This is optional.

syntax_14849.creator: Romitou
syntax_14849.title: Mongo section document
syntax_14849.description: The Mongo section document allows the initialization of a new document and allows to define its fields in a quick and intuitive way with the expression "Mongo section document value". The variable you specify in the first line represents the variable that will contain the document built in the section. You can then use this variable after the end of your section.

en: The Mongo section document allows the initialization of a new document and allows to define its fields in a quick and intuitive way with the expression "Mongo section document value". The variable you specify in the first line represents the variable that will contain the document built in the section. You can then use this variable after the end of your section.

syntax_14850.creator: Romitou
syntax_14850.title: Mongo server
syntax_14850.description: Create a connection to your remote MongoDB host using this expression.This syntax requires to specify a valid MongoDB connection string.Don't forget to include the 'appName' option in order to improve MongoSK reports in case of problems and to identify requests to your server monitoring.You can consult the attached examples or the official documentation: https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/.

en: Create a connection to your remote MongoDB host using this expression.This syntax requires to specify a valid MongoDB connection string.Don't forget to include the 'appName' option in order to improve MongoSK reports in case of problems and to identify requests to your server monitoring.You can consult the attached examples or the official documentation: https://docs.mongodb.com/manual/reference/connection-string/.

syntax_14851.creator: Romitou
syntax_14851.title: Mongo sort
syntax_14851.description: Defines the sortings to be applied to the query. This is optional.

en: Defines the sortings to be applied to the query. This is optional.

syntax_14852.creator: Romitou
syntax_14852.title: New Mongo document
syntax_14852.description: If you want to create a new empty document to specify your data, this expression will do the job.

en: If you want to create a new empty document to specify your data, this expression will do the job.

syntax_14853.creator: Romitou
syntax_14853.title: New Mongo query
syntax_14853.description: This expression will allow you to create a new Mongo query, which you can refine with the many expressions available.

en: This expression will allow you to create a new Mongo query, which you can refine with the many expressions available.

syntax_14854.creator: Romitou
syntax_14854.title: MongoSK Binary
syntax_14854.description: Represents a binary data, which can be used to store large data in a more efficient way.

en: Represents a binary data, which can be used to store large data in a more efficient way.

syntax_14855.creator: Romitou
syntax_14855.title: MongoSK Collection
syntax_14855.description: Represents a Mongo collection, which contains documents. Use the Mongo Collection expression to create this type.

en: Represents a Mongo collection, which contains documents. Use the Mongo Collection expression to create this type.

syntax_14856.creator: Romitou
syntax_14856.title: MongoSK Database
syntax_14856.description: Represents a Mongo database, which contains collections. If you want to create this type, look at the Mongo Database expression.

en: Represents a Mongo database, which contains collections. If you want to create this type, look at the Mongo Database expression.

syntax_14857.creator: Romitou
syntax_14857.title: MongoSK Document
syntax_14857.description: Represents a Mongo (BSON) document, which contains some fields and values. If you want to read fields values, use the Mongo Value expression. Otherwise, you can use the Mongo Query expression to query your Mongo collection for documents.

en: Represents a Mongo (BSON) document, which contains some fields and values. If you want to read fields values, use the Mongo Value expression. Otherwise, you can use the Mongo Query expression to query your Mongo collection for documents.

syntax_14858.creator: Romitou
syntax_14858.title: MongoSK Filter
syntax_14858.description: Represents a filter, which can be used in your queries to target a certain type of data. You can create a filter using the Mongo Filter expression.

en: Represents a filter, which can be used in your queries to target a certain type of data. You can create a filter using the Mongo Filter expression.

syntax_14859.creator: Romitou
syntax_14859.title: MongoSK Query
syntax_14859.description: Allows you to build a complex query to target a very specific type of data with specific options.

en: Allows you to build a complex query to target a very specific type of data with specific options.

syntax_14860.creator: Romitou
syntax_14860.title: MongoSK Server
syntax_14860.description: Represents a remote MongoDB server, with which you can interact. To create this type, use the Mongo Server expression.

en: Represents a remote MongoDB server, with which you can interact. To create this type, use the Mongo Server expression.

syntax_14861.creator: Romitou
syntax_14861.title: MongoSK Sort
syntax_14861.description: Create a sort to first retrieve certain documents according to the specified fields.

en: Create a sort to first retrieve certain documents according to the specified fields.
